channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse — India & Global intelligence dashboard.

item.title: OPEC sees slower demand growth this year as production falls more than 30% on Hormuz closure
item.description: Global demand is facing constraints because supply from the Persian Gulf has been effectively cut off by Iran's blockade of the Strait of the Hormuz.

item.title: Veefin Solutions Q4: Profit Rises 106% QoQ To ₹16 Cr, Revenue Up 27%
item.description: Supply chain financing company Veefin Solutions reported a 106.5% increase in its consolidated net profit to ₹16 Cr in the…

item.title: Amul hikes milk prices by ₹2 per litre across India, effective May 14
item.description: It also stated that member unions have increased the procurement price paid to farmers by ₹30 per kg fat, marking a 3.7% rise compared to May 2025

item.title: NIA arrests Hizb-ul-Mujahideen linked ‘narco-terrorist’ after extradition from Portugal
item.description: According to the NIA, Iqbal Singh alia Shera was a key conspirator and handler of an India-based narco-terror module engaged in smuggling heroin from Pakistan

item.title: DLF Q4 Results: Net profit flat at ₹1,265 crore, revenue falls 42% YoY; ₹8 dividend announced
item.description: DLF reported Q4 FY26 revenue of ₹2,172 crore, an 8% growth sequentially but down 42% YoY. Net profit before JV profits declined 7% sequentially to ₹720 crore, while consolidated PAT rose to ₹1,265 crore, up 5% sequentially but down 2.3% YoY.

item.title: Students, States, coaching centres say NEET-UG cancellation is unfair, stressful
item.description: With no retest date announced yet, students endure anxiety, uncertainty and pressure; demand raised for free transportation to the examination centre for retest

item.title: JPMorgan Reshuffles Investment Bank Leadership
item.description: JPMorgan Chase named Dorothee Blessing, Kevin Foley and Jared Kaye as co-heads of its global investment banking business. Katherine Doherty has more on the shakeup on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Trump administration considering renaming Iran war to Operation Sledgehammer if fragile ceasefire collapses: Report
item.description: As the US and Iran continue to work towards a framework agreement to end the war, which started in late February, Pentagon is now considering to rename the war in Iran. The development comes as President Donald Trump has threatened to resume military strikes if the two sides fail to make a deal

item.title: Iran war accelerates Chinese EV makers’ global expansion — echoing Japan’s 1970s rise - Automotive News
item.description: Iran war accelerates Chinese EV makers’ global expansion — echoing Japan’s 1970s rise Automotive News

item.title: Youth Congress stages protest against NEET paper leak
Night train restrictions to expedite Kollam railway station redevelopment
item.description: The project will now be accelerated through these regulated night blocks and the 12-meter wide and 14-meter long foot overbridge is expected to be completed by September

item.title: Amul hikes milk prices by ₹2 per litre across India due to rising input costs, effective May 14
item.description: Amul has raised milk prices by ₹2 per litre across India, effective May 14, due to increased input costs. This hike may affect food inflation and impact middle-class budgets. The last price increase occurred on May 1, 2025.

item.title: How Anthropic Could Take an OpenAI Supplier Off The Table
item.description: Is a big AI model maker about to take out a supplier to its archrival? That seems possible if a deal we reported on late Tuesday reaches the finish line. Anthropic is in talks to buy developer tools startup Stainless for at least $300 million, according to Stephanie and Katie . The New York startup makes software development kits that make it easier for developers to build applications from a company’s AI models, whi

item.title: Philippine Senate in lockdown after gunshots fired
item.description: Police commandos entered the building where a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had sought refuge.

item.title: Alibaba to exceed planned AI spending and says margin is secondary
item.description: Alibaba plans to spend more than its projected 380 billion yuan on Artificial Intelligence over three years. The company reported quarterly results impacted by significant investments in AI and cloud infrastructure. Despite profit pressures, Alibaba aims for market leadership, prioritising growth over margins. Demand for AI is surging, benefiting Alibaba's Cloud Intelligence Group.

item.title: Cabinet approves ₹2.6 trillion kharif MSP outlay, energy security measures
item.description: The MSP hikes focused heavily on pulses and oilseeds as the government seeks to curb food inflation and reduce import dependence on edible oils and pulses.

item.title: Nvidia's CEO Is a Late Add to Trump's China Trip
item.description: President Donald Trump invited Nvidia co-founder Jensen Huang to join him on his trip to China at the last minute. This brings AI and chips into the spotlight. Tim Cook and Elon Musk also went along. Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern is traveling with the president and reports from Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Israeli strikes on cars in southern Lebanon kill 12, health ministry says
item.description: Two children are among those reported dead after seven cars were struck in separate attacks south of Beirut.

item.title: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes meeting with Xi Jinping
item.description: The leaders of the global superpowers are expected to discuss the war in Iran, tariffs, AI and Taiwan during the two-day visit.

item.title: Hacker checks out Amazon baby monitor, finds mother of all security flaws - Cybernews
item.description: Hacker checks out Amazon baby monitor, finds mother of all security flaws Cybernews

item.title: Epstein abused me while under house arrest, survivor tells US lawmakers
item.description: Roza was among several victims who gave testimony to Democratic lawmakers on Tuesday.

item.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview. MDASH, short for multi-model agentic scanning harness, is designed as a model-agnostic system that uses bespoke AI agents for different vulnerability

item.title: Uttarakhand govt announces fuel-saving measures, cuts ministers’ convoy size by 50%; pushes EV use
item.description: Uttarakhand's chief minister announced fuel and energy conservation measures, including reducing official vehicle fleets by 50% and observing a weekly 'No Vehicle Day'. The state will also promote electric vehicles, restrict foreign travel, and encourage domestic tourism and reduced oil consumption through public awareness campaigns.

item.title: CBI Arrests 5 In NEET-UG 2026 Paper Leak Case, Raids Multiple Locations
item.description: CBI Arrests Five In NEET UG 2026 Paper Leak Case

item.title: SoftBank logs $600 million paper loss on India-listed portfolio in Jan-Mar
item.description: SoftBank's Indian investments faced a significant downturn in the January-March quarter. Several prominent companies like Swiggy and Ola Electric saw their market value drop. This led to substantial paper losses for SoftBank's Vision Fund. Despite overall gains from other investments, the Indian portfolio's performance highlights recent market challenges. SoftBank has been a major investor in India's startup ecosyste

item.title: Lone Star-Owned German Bank IKB Is Exposed to Collapse of UK Mortgage Firm MFS
item.description: A German bank owned by US investment firm Lone Star Funds is among the growing list of lenders exposed to Market Financial Solutions Ltd., the latest example of how the local UK firm’s collapse has spilled across foreign credit markets.

item.title: TVK talking clean politics now practising dirty politics: DMK president M K Stalin
item.description: MK Stalin accuses Vijay-led TVK of “dirty politics” after the party won the Tamil Nadu trust vote with support from rebel AIADMK MLAs.

item.title: Senegal Targets $7.5 Billion Gas Project to End Energy Subsidies
item.description: Senegal’s state-owned oil company said the Yakaar-Teranga gas discovery will cost $7.5 billion to develop, but will help the country slash energy subsidies once on stream.

item.title: Gastrointestinal illness outbreak leaves over 1000 passengers on a UK-operated cruise ship in France after 49 fell ill
item.description: Gastrointestinal illness outbreak leaves over 1000 passengers on a UK-operated cruise ship in France after 49 fell ill

item.title: Consumers Still Face Months of Pain, Piegza Says
item.description: Lindsey Piegza, Stifel chief economist, says inflation is still a concern for the US economy. She says consumers face months of pain ahead and more price pressures are coming. She speaks on Bloomberg Surveillance. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Uber's India data centre bet; Cyber cover meets AI risk
item.description: Uber will establish its first data centre in India with the Adani Group. This and more in today’s ETtech Top 5.

item.title: Political turmoil in UK as PM Starmer refuses to resign amid growing calls over his future: Here's all you need to know
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is facing mounting pressure to resign after Labour’s local election losses triggered calls for his exit from ministers, MPs and unions. Despite growing unrest within the party, Starmer has insisted he will remain in office and continue governing.

item.title: PM Modi leads by example: Convoy cuts and fuel conservation sweep across India; HM Amit Shah, UP CM Yogi announce cuts
item.description: In response to Prime Minister Modi's appeal for energy conservation, several officials, including Governors and Chief Ministers, have reduced their convoys by up to 50%.

item.title: Pakistan Growth Accelerates Even as Iran War Raises Risks
item.description: Pakistan’s economy accelerated in the last quarter even as the rising global crude prices due to the Iran conflict cloud the outlook for the nation, which imports most of its fuel requirement.

item.title: Hindustan Petroleum Q4 net profit accelerates 46% on improved refining margins before conflict impact
item.description: Mumbai-headquartered refiner did not provide a guidance amidst the volatility emanating because of the conflict in West Asia

item.title: Alibaba's core profit plunges 84% as CEO says return on AI investments are 'extremely clear'
item.description: Alibaba on Wednesday said its core profitability plunged in the March quarter amid heavy investments in tech and e-commerce.

item.title: Predulive Labs: The Physical AI Revolution in Infrastructure Safety - Indian Startup Times
item.description: Predulive Labs: The Physical AI Revolution in Infrastructure Safety Indian Startup Times

item.title: Cybersecurity firm Exaforce raises $125 million from HarbourVest, Peak XV, Khosla Ventures
item.description: Cybersecurity firm Exaforce secures $125 million in funding. This capital will enhance its AI-SOC platform and global expansion. The company's real-time security knowledge graph allows rapid threat investigations. This technology aims to shift leverage to defenders. Exaforce serves major sectors like healthcare and finance. Customers report accelerated threat detection and response.

item.title: AIADMK internal crisis deepens: EPS removes rebel leaders from party posts for backing Tamil Nadu CM Vijay in floor test
item.description: AIADMK's internal conflict escalates as party chief Palaniswami expels rebel leaders. These leaders had backed Tamil Nadu CM Vijay during a crucial trust vote. The move follows accusations of weakening the party's anti-DMK stance and exploring alliances. This leadership struggle intensifies ahead of future elections.

item.title: India commemorates “Operation Sindoor” as a response to cross-border terrorism - Seychelles News Agency
item.description: India commemorates “Operation Sindoor” as a response to cross-border terrorism Seychelles News Agency
